caseResults.validationSummary: The screenshot shows a marketplace product listing page, not an order confirmation page. No order has been placed, no confirmation is visible, no order number exists, and no phone product is present in the catalog.
improvements: Inspect the actual DOM on the homepage after navigation to enumerate all navigation link texts and ARIA roles; replace the hardcoded selector 'role=link[name=/phones/i]' with a broader candidate list that includes text-based selectors such as 'text=/phones/i', 'a:has-text("Phones")', '[data-testid*="phone"]', and 'nav a' enumeration so the fallback resolver can locate the correct element
Add a DOM-dump diagnostic step at the start of the test that logs all visible navigation links (innerText + href) to a file, enabling accurate selector discovery without repeated blind runs

Validate that a 'Phones' category actually exists in the product catalog before attempting navigation; the validation report confirms the catalog contains headphones, speaker, smartwatch, desk lamp, thermal bottle, and keyboard — no phone product exists, so the test precondition 'At least one phone product is available in the store' is unmet and must be resolved at the data/environment level before automation can pass

Introduce a precondition guard at the top of the test that queries the product catalog API or page for a phone product and skips with a descriptive message if none is found, preventing false failures caused by missing test data

Broaden the category navigation selector fallback chain to include: page.getByRole('link', { name: /phone/i }), page.locator('a[href*="phone"]'), page.locator('[class*="category"]').filter({ hasText: /phone/i }), and page.locator('nav').getByText(/phone/i) to cover varied markup patterns

After clicking the category link, add an explicit wait for a product grid or list locator (e.g., '[data-testid="product-card"]' or '.product-list') to be visible before proceeding, replacing any implicit timing assumptions

Split the single monolithic end-to-end test into guarded checkpoints — homepage load, category navigation, product detail, add-to-cart, checkout, and order confirmation — each with individual assertions and screenshots, so failures are pinpointed to the exact step rather than surfacing only at the first unresolved locator

Add a post-order-confirmation assertion that checks for an order number pattern (e.g., /order[\s#]*\w+/i) using page.getByText with a regex, and assert its visibility with a meaningful timeout of at least 10 seconds to account for async confirmation rendering

Capture a full-page screenshot and page.content() snapshot immediately after each major navigation step and attach them as Playwright test attachments to provide richer debugging context beyond the single failure screenshot currently available

caseResults.validationSummary: The screenshot shows the checkout page with a form and 'Place order' button visible, and a cart notification confirming a product was added. However, several criteria cannot be verified from this single screenshot: homepage with product listings, product detail/add-to-cart visibility, cart page details, and order confirmation after purchase. Additionally, the product added is 'Aurora Wireless Headphones' which is not a phone, partially misaligning with the user story. No payment details field is visible in the checkout form.
improvements: Expand the shipping address field selector candidates to include additional patterns observed in real checkout forms: add 'input[name*="street"]', 'input[name*="shipping"]', 'input[autocomplete="street-address"]', 'input[autocomplete="address-line1"]', 'textarea[name*="address"]', and '[data-testid*="address"]' to the fallback selector array for the shipping address resolver

Before attempting to fill the address field, take a DOM snapshot or use 'page.$$eval("input", els => els.map(e => ({name:e.name,id:e.id,placeholder:e.placeholder})))' to log all visible input attributes at checkout, enabling precise selector discovery and preventing locator resolution failures

Add a dedicated page-object or helper for the checkout form that dynamically discovers form fields by label text using 'page.getByLabel()' or 'page.locator("label").filter({hasText:/address/i}).locator(".. input")' as primary strategy, falling back to attribute-based selectors only when label matching fails

Fix product category navigation to target a phone product specifically: add an assertion after product selection that the product name or category contains keywords like 'phone', 'smartphone', or 'mobile' to prevent purchasing unrelated items (e.g., 'Aurora Wireless Headphones') and align with the user story intent

Add explicit wait and screenshot steps at each major flow transition (homepage → category → product detail → cart → checkout → confirmation) using 'await page.waitForLoadState("networkidle")' and 'await page.screenshot()' calls to provide visual evidence for each acceptance criterion instead of a single terminal screenshot

Implement a checkout form field discovery step that checks for payment section visibility: assert that at least one payment-related field ('input[name*="card"]', 'input[name*="payment"]', '[data-testid*="payment"]', or an iframe containing payment fields like Stripe) exists before attempting payment entry, and add a conditional handler for embedded payment iframes

Add a robust 'Phones' category selector with multiple fallbacks: '[data-category="phones"]', 'a:has-text("Phones")', 'nav >> text=Phones', '[href*="phone"]', '[href*="mobile"]' and assert that the resulting product grid contains items before clicking, to prevent silent navigation failures that lead to wrong product selection

After clicking 'Place Order', add an explicit wait for the confirmation page using 'page.waitForURL(/confirmation|order-success|thank-you/i)' or 'page.waitForSelector("text=/order number|order confirmed|thank you/i")' with a 15-second timeout, and capture the order number value to assert it is non-empty, covering the currently UNKNOWN order confirmation criterion

Separate the 16-step test into smaller scoped fixtures or test hooks: use 'test.beforeEach' to handle login and cart clearing, so that locator failures during checkout are isolated from setup issues and retry logic applies only to the failing step rather than the entire flow
